page: Microsoft Azure FX12-6mds_v2 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the FXmdsv2 family. It features 6 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The physical layout consists of 3 physical cores with 2 threads per core. Memory capacity is exceptionally high at 252.0 GB, yielding a high resource density of 42.0 GB of RAM per core. The server includes 945 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage, while containing no GPUs.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core performance, with the Geekbench single-core score and single-threaded gzip compression both placing in the top tier (top 10%). Single-threaded bzip3 decompression is also strong, ranking in the top 25%. However, multi-core and multi-threaded metrics, including stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, Geekbench multi-core, and bzip3 decompression multi-threaded, fall into the average category (middle 50%). Memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks, along with PassMark memory and database operations, also register in the middle 50%.

The server exhibits a clear tradeoff between high single-core performance and moderate multi-core scaling due to its 6-vCPU configuration. While LLM inference text generation for small models is strong (top 25%), performance drops to weak (bottom 25%) for medium and large models. Qualitatively, the high memory-to-vCPU ratio represents a specialized resource density profile that is cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads rather than compute-heavy parallel processing. This configuration is suitable for memory-intensive applications, database operations, and text processing tasks that benefit from high single-thread speeds and large memory footprints.
description: Microsoft Azure FX12-6mds_v2 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server from the FXmdsv2 family, featuring 6 dedicated vCPUs on an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz. It is equipped with 252.0 GB of RAM, providing 42.0 GB per core, alongside 945 GB of local storage and no GPU. Benchmarks show top-tier (top 10%) single-core performance in Geekbench and gzip compression, while multi-core metrics, memory bandwidth, and database operations remain in the average tier. The server presents a high memory-to-vCPU ratio, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound tasks rather than highly parallelized compute workloads. LLM inference text generation for small models is strong, though performance drops significantly for larger models. It is suited for memory-intensive applications, database operations, and text processing workloads requiring high single-thread performance.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX12-6mds_v2 is a memory-optimized server featuring 6 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 252 GB of RAM, and 945 GB of local storage, delivering top-tier single-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure FX12-6mds_v2 is a memory-optimized server with 6 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 252 GB of RAM, 945 GB of local storage, and high single-core performance.
tagline: A memory-intensive server combining high single-core performance with an exceptional memory-to-vCPU ratio and local storage for demanding database workloads.

252.0 GB of RAM providing 42.0 GB per core
945 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier single-core CPU and text processing performance
Suited for memory-intensive applications and database operations
